entry.title: python迭代器
entry.content:
迭代器

迭代器是迭代取值的工具，每次的结果都依赖于上一次结果。它是一种不依赖索引的取值方法。

迭代器的定义

  1. 实现了__iter__和__next__方法的类属于迭代器
  2. __iter__: 返回self
  3. __next__: 返回下一个数据，如果没有数据了的话，抛出StopIteration异常。并且其取值不依赖索引。
# define classclass iteration(object):  def __init__(self):    self.counter = 0  def __iter__(self):    return self  def __next__(self):    self.counter += 1    if self.counter == 4:      raise StopIteration()    return self.counter# instantiationv1 = iteration()print(v1.__next__())print(next(v1))print(v1.__next__())print(next(v1))'''output123--StopIteration--'''

注：v1.__next__()可用next(v1)代替，两者等价

for循环与迭代器

for循环的本质就是迭代器的循环，下面介绍for循环的原理：

  1. for循环接收一个迭代器。
  2. 首先会调用迭代器内的iter方法返回self
  3. 调用迭代器中的next，并赋值给for中的变量名item
  4. 循环直到迭代器抛出StopIteration异常，for会自动捕获异常并结束循环
for item in iteration():  print(item)'''output123'''

for可迭代对象：凡是可被for循环的对象都为可迭代对象

可迭代对象

迭代器可以用于for中，其中的特殊的next方法是遍历的核心。但我们又注意到for循环首先会执行iter并返回带有next方法的迭代器self，然后再调用next。所以使用for的关键是有没有iter方法和iter方法返回中有没有next方法。所以一个类中定义了iter方法并返回一个迭代器对象，他也可以用于for中，我们把这种类成为可迭代对象。

# define iteratiable classclass iterable_ob(object):    def __iter__(self):        return iteration()# instantiationv2 = iterable_ob()for item in v2:    print(item)'''output123'''

迭代器可迭代对象：迭代器一定是可迭代对象，但可迭代对象不一定是迭代器

可迭代对象实例：range()

python中有许多函数都为可迭代对象。我们以range()为例，检查其是否为可迭代对象。为此我们要检查range()有无iter以及iter是否返回迭代器。

  1. 检查是否有iter

首先我们可以查看range()中变量方法

dir(range(10))'''['__bool__', '__class__', '__contains__', '__delattr__', '__dir__', '__doc__', '__eq__', '__format__', '__ge__', '__getattribute__', '__getitem__', '__gt__', '__hash__', '__init__', '__init_subclass__', '__iter__', '__le__', '__len__', '__lt__', '__ne__', '__new__', '__reduce__', '__reduce_ex__', '__repr__', '__reversed__', '__setattr__', '__sizeof__', '__str__', '__subclasshook__', 'count', 'index', 'start', 'step', 'stop']'''

可以看到，range()中有iter方法，可能为一个可迭代对象。

  1. 检查iter是否返回迭代器

通过直接调用iter方法得到其返回，然后检查返回中是否符合迭代器特征。

dir(range(10).__iter__())'''output['__class__', '__delattr__', '__dir__', '__doc__', '__eq__', '__format__', '__ge__', '__getattribute__', '__gt__', '__hash__', '__init__', '__init_subclass__', '__iter__', '__le__', '__length_hint__', '__lt__', '__ne__', '__new__', '__next__', '__reduce__', '__reduce_ex__', '__repr__', '__setattr__', '__setstate__', '__sizeof__', '__str__', '__subclasshook__'] '''

可以看到，返回值中有iter和next方法，所以range()是可迭代对象。

判断是否为迭代器或可迭代对象

python中内置两个函数供我们方便的检查一个实例是否为迭代器与可迭代对象。

from collections.abc import Iterable, Iteratorable = iterable_ob()ation = iteration()print(isinstance(able, Iterable))print(isinstance(able, Iterator))print(isinstance(ation, Iterable))print(isinstance(ation, Iterator))'''outputTrueFalseTrueTrue'''

优点与缺点

优点：

  1. 不依靠索引
  2. 一次只取一个值，节省内存

缺点：

  1. 只能从前往后取，不能去特定值，不够灵活
  2. 无法得知迭代器长度

Reference

python doc

bilibili_15分钟彻底搞懂迭代器、可迭代对象、生成器【python迭代器】

blog_python迭代器

知乎

生成器

entry.title: NLP 工具大赏
entry.content:
NLTK

NLTK官网

停用词库

from nltk.corpus import stopwordsstopwords.words('english')
['i', 'me', 'my', 'myself', 'we', 'our', 'ours', 'ourselves', 'you', "you're", "you've", "you'll", "you'd", 'your', 'yours', 'yourself', 'yourselves', 'he', 'him', 'his', 'himself', 'she', "she's", 'her', 'hers', 'herself', 'it', "it's", 'its', 'itself', 'they', 'them', 'their', 'theirs', 'themselves', 'what', 'which', 'who', 'whom', 'this', 'that', "that'll", 'these', 'those', 'am', 'is', 'are', 'was', 'were', 'be', 'been', 'being', 'have', 'has', 'had', 'having', 'do', 'does', 'did', 'doing', 'a', 'an', 'the', 'and', 'but', 'if', 'or', 'because', 'as', 'until', 'while', 'of', 'at', 'by', 'for', 'with', 'about', 'against', 'between', 'into', 'through', 'during', 'before', 'after', 'above', 'below', 'to', 'from', 'up', 'down', 'in', 'out', 'on', 'off', 'over', 'under', 'again', 'further', 'then', 'once', 'here', 'there', 'when', 'where', 'why', 'how', 'all', 'any', 'both', 'each', 'few', 'more', 'most', 'other', 'some', 'such', 'no', 'nor', 'not', 'only', 'own', 'same', 'so', 'than', 'too', 'very', 's', 't', 'can', 'will', 'just', 'don', "don't", 'should', "should've", 'now', 'd', 'll', 'm', 'o', 're', 've', 'y', 'ain', 'aren', "aren't", 'couldn', "couldn't", 'didn', "didn't", 'doesn', "doesn't", 'hadn', "hadn't", 'hasn', "hasn't", 'haven', "haven't", 'isn', "isn't", 'ma', 'mightn', "mightn't", 'mustn', "mustn't", 'needn', "needn't", 'shan', "shan't", 'shouldn', "shouldn't", 'wasn', "wasn't", 'weren', "weren't", 'won', "won't", 'wouldn', "wouldn't"]

常用语料库

未标注库

from nltk.corpus import gutenberggutenberg.raw('austen-emma.txt')

标注库

from nltk.corpus import sententce_polarity

词典

from nltk.corpus import wordnetsyns = wordnet.synsets('bank')syns'''[Synset('bank.n.01'), Synset('depository_financial_institution.n.01'), Synset('bank.n.03'), Synset('bank.n.04'), Synset('bank.n.05'), Synset('bank.n.06'), Synset('bank.n.07'), Synset('savings_bank.n.02'), Synset('bank.n.09'), Synset('bank.n.10'), Synset('bank.v.01'), Synset('bank.v.02'), Synset('bank.v.03'), Synset('bank.v.04'), Synset('bank.v.05'), Synset('deposit.v.02'), Synset('bank.v.07'), Synset('trust.v.01')]'''
syns[0].name()  # 'bank.n.01'syns[0].definition()  # 'sloping land (especially the slope beside a body of water)'syns[0].examples()  # ['they pulled the canoe up on the bank', 'he sat on the bank of the river and watched the currents']syns[0].hypernyms()  # [Synset('slope.n.01')]dog = wordnet.synsets('dog')cat = wordnet.synsets('cat')dog[0].wup_similarity(cat[0])  # 0.8571428571428571

分句

from nltk.tokenize import sent_tokenizetext = gutenberg.raw('austen-emma.txt')sent = sent_tokenize(text)sent[99]  # 'I meant
only myself.'sent[100]  # 'Mr. Knightley loves to find fault with me, you know--
in a joke--it is all a joke.'sent[101]  # 'We always say what we like to one another."'sent[102]  # 'Mr. Knightley, in fact, was one of the few people who could see
faults in Emma Woodhouse, and the only one who ever told her of them:
and though this was not particularly agreeable to Emma herself,
she knew it would be so much less so to her father, that she would
not have him really suspect such a circumstance as her not being
thought perfect by every body.'

分词

from nltk.tokenize import word_tokenizeword_tokenize(sent[100])  # ['Mr.', 'Knightley', 'loves', 'to', 'find', 'fault', 'with', 'me', ',', 'you', 'know', '--', 'in', 'a', 'joke', '--', 'it', 'is', 'all', 'a', 'joke', '.']

词性标注

from nltk import pos_tagpos_tag(word_tokenize('they sat by the fire'))  # [('they', 'PRP'), ('sat', 'VBP'), ('by', 'IN'), ('the', 'DT'), ('fire', 'NN')]pos_tag(word_tokenize('they fire a gun'))  # [('they', 'PRP'), ('fire', 'VBP'), ('a', 'DT'), ('gun', 'NN')]
查看具体pos含义
import nltknltk.help.upenn_tagset()

entry.title: The Continuity Equation
entry.content:
The Continuity Equation

One of important fundamental law is that mass is conserve. We conclude it in this theorem.

Continuity equation

Theorem

Let and be the density and the velocity fields respectively of the fluid, so that is a scalar field and is a vector field. Consider an arbritrary fixed closed surface drawn in the fluid. Then

entry.title: Material Derivative
entry.content:
Material Derivative

Usually we use [[Fields/Fluid Machanics/Description#Eulerian description|Eulerian description]] to represent fluid. However, if we want to calculate some value such as acceleration which have well defined on [[Fields/Fluid Machanics/Description#Lagrangian description|Lagrangian description]], we cannot differential it directly. Hence, we need find a new method to gives the Lagrangian time change in terms of Eulerian measurement.

The formula is

Proof

We proof by connect two equation. The one is from definition, other is Taylor’s theorem

Hence,

Dividing both sides of this equation by and appling the limit , we have

Acceleration

The convective derivative also applies to velocity. The acceleration of a fluid particle (following the particle) is

#TODO The mean

Reference

深入理解流体力学中的加速度和物质导数_理论_科普_流体基础_其他软件-仿真秀干货文章 (fangzhenxiu.com)

entry.title: Orientation
entry.content:
Orientation

There are three definition of orientation:

  • A surface is said to be oriented if a unit normal vector is constructed at each point of the surface in such way that the vector change continuously.
  • A unit normal vector is said to be orientation of the surface if the surface is oriented.
  • The surface is said to be orientable if it can be oriented by construct a unit normal vector.

One vector have two orientation, and the orientation of surface is depend on the component sign of [[Fields/Integral/Surface Integral#Normal vector|normal vector]].

The orientation of surface is a relative concept.

For curve, we judge orientation by right hand principle.
![[Fields/Integral/attachments/Screen Shot 2021-12-08 at 13.03.17.png|300]]

entry.title: Stoke's Theorem
entry.content:
Stoke’s Theorem

Given a vector field, the theorem relates the integral of the curl of the vector field over some surface, to the line integral of the vector field around the boundary of the surface

This is the high dimension version of [[Fields/Integral/Green’s Theorem|Green’s theorem]]

Theorem

If is an [[Fields/Integral/Orientation|oriented surface]], bounded by a single smooth parametric curve . If the components of vector field

has continuous partial derivative on some open set containing , then

where the integral is taken in the positive direction of .

Exercise

verify the stoke’s theorem

In stoke’s ways: we could always find curl of vector field and normal vector by given surface. Pay attention to choose proper orientation of normal vector.

When we solve relate problem, we do not need calculate unit normal vector but normal vector since the unit part is diminish in the calculation.

In line integral ways: change to parameter representation and find derivative of curve.

#

Reference

格林定理推广到stokes定理 - 知乎 (zhihu.com)
Calculus III - Stokes’ Theorem (lamar.edu)

entry.title: Line integrals independent of the path
Description
entry.content:
Description

To analysis fluid mechanics in continuous field, we need to model it. There are two describe method.

First, we introduce the definition of two description and what they focus on. Secondly, the method of calculate representation line on them will be mention. Finally, we state what different and similarity of them

Lagrangian description

Lagrangian description focus on point or particles. It provide the history movement of a particle of the fluid.

Where the in the left side is represent the history of movement. is the position of the fluid particle observed at time .

There are some definition about this formula

  • fluid particle: A point which move with the velocity of the fluid at that point.
  • Material coordinate: The coordinate of the fluid particle at zero type.

This satisfy the definition of coordinate. If independent, it represent all initial point; if independent, it represent a particular line.

Pathlines

The pathline is defined by the locus or trajectory followed by any given fluid particle for a given time interval.

Relation to velocity field

It can be obtained by .

When integral, the lower bound and upper bound of path and time is: , . At last, we need the equation on x-y plane, hence we eliminate by substitution.

Eulerian description

Eulerian description focus on the whole space with field. For example, the velocity field.

Streamlines

Streamlines represent the loci that are tangent to the velocity vector in vector field at a particular time.
![[Fields/Fluid Machanics/attachments/Screen Shot 2021-12-09 at 14.37.40.png|300]]

To find the streamline, we use the relation tangent:

where is a small displacement along the streamline.

Hence we have

Integral this formula gives the family of streamlines.

If the streamline intersect each other, that’s mean the vector would have two direction. However, it is no physical significant to it. Hence, the only condition of intersection is the zero point, named stagnation points or critical points.

There are some notion to solve relate problems.

  1. When integral the equation, regard be constant
  2. just add integral notation
  3. choose a proper constant integral constant
  4. to find a special set, set a particular point

Difference

Eulerian description observe general point in the fluid but not seek directly way to trace the path of particular point.

Lagrangian description input: initial point coordinate, time; output: coordinate of this point; function:

Eulerian description input: coordinate and time; output: velocity; function: field

However, those two describe does not depend on the choose of coordinate, but the observer

Reference

流体力学学习笔记之一：描述连续介质的两种观点 – Xin He (wordpress.com)

深入理解流体力学中的加速度和物质导数_理论_科普_流体基础_其他软件-仿真秀干货文章 (fangzhenxiu.com)

entry.title: Divergence and curl of a vector field
entry.content:
Divergence and curl of a vector field

The [[Fields/Fields/Gradient|gradient]] operation with [[Fields/Fields/Scalar and Vector Field#Vector Field|vector field]]

Divergence of a vector field

The scalar product between and a vector field , is .
This is called the divergence of the vector field (often written as div ) and in Cartesian coordinates is explicitly given by

The divergence of a vector field is a [[Fields/Fields/Scalar and Vector Field#Scalar Field | scalar field]].

Please note, as is a vector operator and not a vector, then .

irrotational

A vector field with zero curl is said to be conservative. We also say that such a field is irrotational.

Physical significance

If , out > in
https://www.zhihu.com/question/46380810

Curl of a vector field

The vector product between and a vector field , is or .
This is called the curl of the vector field (often written as curl ) and in Cartesian coordinates is explicitly given by

The curl of a vector field is a [[Fields/Fields/Scalar and Vector Field#Vector Field|vector field]].

Please note, as is a vector operator,then

solenoidal

A vector field with zero divergence is said to be solenoidal.

Formula

Let and be two differential scalar fields and let and be two differentiable vector fields. First order differentials

Addition satisfy linear all time

Dot multiplication

  1. .
    Dot just like derivative

Cross multiplication

Second order differentials


  4. where

Model

In the case of the second order differential operator formulae, the first identity, i.e., div grad , is an important operator that occurs in many physical problems. It is called the Laplacian and is denoted by or where

Laplace’s equation is where the Laplacian is equal to zero, i.e., the equation

This equation models:

  1. The distribution of electrostatic potential in a uniform dielectric material or free space where there is no charge.
  2. The distribution of temperature for steady states.
  3. The distribution of an inviscid, irrotational fluid flow.

Poisson’s Equation is a variation of Laplace’s equation where the right hand side is no longer zero, i.e.,

The equation also occurs widely and models

  1. Unsteady heat flow.
  2. The solution of Gauss’s law for a conservative field.

In other coordinate system

Cartesian system

Let and , then

cylindrical system

Let and , then

spherical system

Let and , then
